Where your blunders come from

Each blunder bucketed by the engine evaluation (your point of view) on the move just before it. Blunders thrown from winning or equal positions are the ones you can most directly stop.

Study plan

Phase priorities (worst leak first)

  1. Endgame: avg CPL 88, 86 blunders
  2. Middlegame: avg CPL 81, 73 blunders
  3. Opening: avg CPL 49, 19 blunders

Openings to shore up

  • Kings Pawn Opening 1...e5 (white): 18-9-0, opening CPL 62
  • Philidor Defense 3.Bc4 (black): 3-5-0, opening CPL 48
  • Bishops Opening (black): 1-4-0, opening CPL 60
  • Kings Pawn Opening 1...e5 (black): 2-1-0, opening CPL 77
  • Philidor Defense (black): 2-1-0, opening CPL 67
  • Kings Pawn Opening (white): 1-1-0, opening CPL 77
  • Philidor Defense 3.Nc3 (black): 0-2-0, opening CPL 30
  • Philidor Defense Exchange Variation (black): 1-0-1, opening CPL 80

Replay these positions

Set each up and find the move you missed.

  • 24...Rcb8
  • 34...Kf4
  • 25...Bxa4
  • 24...Qxg2
  • 26...Kxb6

Where to practice

  • Lichess puzzles — daily tactics build the habit of checking your opponent's reply before you move, which is where most blunders are avoided.
  • Lichess practice drills — the endgame is one of your two leakiest phases; these teach the basic winning and drawing techniques.
  • Lichess opening explorer — look up Bishops Opening (black), your weakest opening, to learn its main lines and plans.

Glossary

CPL
Centipawn loss: how much worse your move was than the engine's best move, in hundredths of a pawn. Lower is better.
blunder
A move that throws away a lot — here, 300 or more centipawns versus the best move.
mistake
A move that loses a moderate amount, 100 to 299 centipawns.
inaccuracy
A small slip, 50 to 99 centipawns worse than the best move.
time trouble
Moves made with little time left on your clock, where mistakes are more likely.
evaluation
The engine's score for a position in centipawns (100 = one pawn). Positive means you are better.
phase
Which stage of the game a move is in: opening, middlegame, or endgame.
